etymology.description: Depicts a trident-like weapon -> similar to a three-bladed polearm -> borrowed for pronoun use
etymology.interpretation: 我 (wǒ, "I") was originally a pictogram of a trident-like weapon or tool, later borrowed phonetically to represent the pronoun meaning "I" due to its sound. The image of a multi-bladed weapon serves as the foundation, with usage evolving through phonetic borrowing.

examples.chinese: 我喜欢读书(我喜歡讀書)。
我有很多朋友(我有很多朋友)。
我吃了早餐(我吃了早餐)，然后去上班(然後去上班)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ chīle zǎocān, ránhòu qù shàngbān.
examples.english: I had breakfast, then went to work.
